description: Declares collaboration topology and launch/dispatch gates.

supportedPriorityOwners.description: Legacy priority slug allow-list. Omitted, empty, or ["*"] means the route may launch any active priority.

initialLanes.description: Optional bootstrap lane set. When present, live launch starts only these lanes; additional lanes must be added through a validated topology option.

topologyOptions.description: Validated lane-group options Oscar may request after bootstrap. Options constrain add-lane requests; they are not free-form topology invention.

orchestratorCommit.description: Route-owned commit policy. When enabled, the route stages exact files from the accepted writer result and commits on the writer lane's behalf. Optional laneWriteScopes entries may narrow a named writer lane to explicit allowed/excluded repo-relative path patterns.

gitCommitPolicy.description: Explicit writer-lane git commit requirement for routes that still expect a lane to run git commits itself. Launch preflight blocks when the lane lacks git-commit capability.

leadRescue.description: Route-owned result supersession policy. When allowed, named lead lanes may supersede named non-PASS teammate lanes through immutable supersession records.

leadSupportCommit.description: Guarded lead-lane support commit policy. When enabled, a declared lead may commit exact files inside explicit support scopes to clear mechanical orchestration blockers without a teammate result artifact.

implementationOwnership.description: Implementation provenance policy. When enabled or omitted, implementation-surface commits require the configured owner lane result artifact, normally Bob through the route/profile snapshot adapter.

modelRoles.description: Optional route-level overrides for profile modelRoles. Used when a route needs a different planning, research, build, or synthesis model assignment without rewriting the base profile.

rules: Routes declare topology; profiles assign models.
Priority slug allow-lists are optional route constraints, not a priority validity source.
Launch fails if route lanes are absent from the selected profile.
One-writer routes allow only the configured writer lane to modify repo files.
Orchestrator-owned commits must be declared per route and use exact-file staging from accepted result artifacts.

When orchestratorCommit.laneWriteScopes is present, each lane scope narrows that lane's commit authority to explicit allowed/excluded repo-relative paths; lanes without a scope continue to use the resolved priority write boundary.
Routes that require writer-lane git commits must declare gitCommitPolicy.mode=writer-lane with writerLanes; launch preflight checks git-commit capability before starting lanes.
Lead-rescue supersession must be route-declared with leadRescue.allowed=true, lead lanes, and superseded lanes, unless an immutable supersession record cites founder authorization.
Lead support commits are for bounded orchestration support only: exact files, declared lead lanes, declared allowed/excluded scopes, no run-local artifacts, no unrelated staged files.
Implementation-surface commits must trace to the configured implementation owner lane result artifact unless route policy explicitly disables or exempts that surface.
Lead-driven teammate lanes wait for a concrete lead dispatch unless the route explicitly sets allowAutonomousTeammateStart.

laneRequirements.<lane>.adapterSandbox may explicitly override adapter sandbox launch mode for a lane; use it only when the lane's authorized work needs OS resources a workspace sandbox blocks, and keep write boundaries/result evidence intact.

Bootstrap routes may declare initialLanes to start Oscar first; every later counterpart lane must be covered by a topologyOptions entry and pass profile, adapter, boundary, git-state, helper, watcher, and result-contract checks.
No-priority routes must start idle and wait for a concrete task.
Route modelRoles override profile modelRoles but do not authorize silent role substitution; fallback and degraded-mode behavior remains explicit.
